textPlain: Doesn’t surprise me at all that people who know what they’re doing are building their own images with nix for ML. Tens of millions of dollars have been wasted in the past 2 years by teams who are too stupid to upgrade from buggy software bundled into their “golden” docker container, or too slow to upgrade their drivers/kernels/toolkits. It’s such a shame. It’s not that hard.Edit: see also the horrors that exist when you mix nvidia software versions: https://developer.nvidia.com/blog/cuda-c-compiler-updates-im...
Great, can't wait for the systemd crew come out with: Docker was Too Slow, So We Replaced It: Systemd in Production [asciinema]
New corollary: sometimes new tech gets built because you don't know how to correctly use existing tech.
Some people, when confronted with a problem, think "I know, I'll use Nix." Now they have two problems.
Alternative: just produce relocatable builds that don’t require all of this unnecessary extra infrastructure
I use Nix and like it, but in terms of DX docker is still miles ahead.  I liken it to Python vs Rust. Use the right tool.
Can you be explicit in how the dollars are being wasted? Maybe it's obvious to you but omjow does an old kernel waste money?
Please elaborate. How does one "just" do that?

Docker is overkill if all you really need is app packaging.Docker containers may not be portable anyway when the CUDA version used in the container has to match the kernel driver and GPU firmware, etc.
